entities.name: Krung Thai Bank Public Company Limited

financial.note: Holds ~55.07% stake through the Financial Institutions Development Fund (FIDF); KTB acts as the primary vehicle for government financial policy and stimulus disbursement.
2026 BoT policy rates (at ~1.25%) directly dictate Net Interest Margins (NIM); KTB targets a 2.35-2.5% NIM for 2026 amid a 'Neutral' carry environment.
Holds ~3.24% stake; provides capital stability and aligns with government-linked institutional investment strategies.

supply_chain.note: IBM holds 90% of the KTBCS joint venture; critical for KTB's 2026 migration from legacy systems to a secure, hybrid cloud-based 'Future Banking' architecture.
51/49 JV focusing on AI-driven banking and cloud engineering to support the 'Pao Tang' ecosystem expansion.
Serves >40-50 million users; the primary channel for retail credit (KTC), government bonds (USD Bond Wallet), and state welfare stimulus.

competitors.note: Primary rival for large-corporate lending and regional transaction banking.
Direct competition for mass-retail deposits and policy-driven lending mandates.
Competes in digital banking innovation and retail platform ecosystems.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: Persistently high debt (~90% of GDP) in 2026 remains a ceiling for retail loan growth and a primary driver for credit cost provisions (target 75-115bp).
As the state’s fiscal agent, KTB captures transactional fees and massive deposit inflows during stimulus distribution cycles.
Influences the success of the 'USD Bond Wallet' and cross-border trade finance; a weaker THB bolsters the value of foreign-denominated assets.
